title: NH athlete is running Boston for the care that keeps her breathing
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Alison Clode, 50, from Portsmouth, NH, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass Eye and Ear.
Clode undergoes treatment at Mass Eye and Ear for subglottic stenosis, which affects her ability to breathe.
She aims to raise funds for Dr. Song's research at the Otolaryngology unit through her marathon run.

title: A major North Shore office park is adding 60 new condos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cummings Center in Beverly adds 60 condos, targeting 55-plus buyers seeking to downsize.
80% of new condos reserved for households with a resident aged 55 or older.
Condominiums range 700-2,600 sq ft, priced $500,000-$1.5 million; nine units subsidized.

title: Connelly Early named to Red Sox starting rotation
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Connelly Early named to Red Sox rotation, starting third game against the Reds on Sunday, March 26.
Early, 23, joined Red Sox in 2023 draft and debuted in 2025, matching a rookie strikeout record.
Johan Oviedo will start the season in the bullpen; team's rotation decisions to be reevaluated soon.

title: Experience an ‘unforgettable’ Easter weekend trip in this Maine city, according to Booking.com
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Portland, Maine, was named among 15 unforgettable Easter weekend trips in the U.S. by Booking.com.
Travelers in Portland can enjoy waterfront activities, seafood dining, and historic sites during Easter weekend.
Portland hotels and chefs have been recognized by Michelin keys and James Beard Foundation in recent years.

title: Netflix star Emma Hernan is opening a frozen food facility in Hingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emma Hernan, of 'Selling Sunset', opens a $4.6 million frozen food facility in Hingham for Coast to Coast Kitchen.
The 28,000-square-foot facility will produce crab cakes, shrimp spring rolls, and employ about 50 people.
Hernan's family, already with significant local operations, emphasizes hiring local women at the new facility.

title: This mental health worker is running Boston for her neighbors in South Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sarah Clark, a mental health worker, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon supporting South Boston Neighborhood House.
Clark's work focuses on providing mental health resources to youth in Boston and surrounding areas like Quincy and Lynn.
South Boston Neighborhood House offers education, job, and security services, impacting local families for 125 years.

title: Giveaway: Enter to win tickets to ‘The Drama’
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Enter the giveaway to win tickets to 'The Drama,' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, by submitting a relationship question.
Email submissions for the contest open March 24 at 10 a.m. and close March 27 at 11 a.m. EST.
Winners will be chosen by 5 p.m. March 27 and receive tickets to a screening in Somerville on Sunday.

title: Lowell was named the best place to live in Mass. But why?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lowell named best place to live in Massachusetts and No. 3 in the Northeast by Livability.
Recognition due to strong sense of community, vibrant arts scene, and expanding opportunities.
Key features include Lowell Folk Festival, educational institutions, and innovative development projects.

title: Body of missing Holy Cross student recovered in Connecticut lake
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dominick Tocci, 20, was found dead after a canoe accident on Lake Hayward, East Haddam, Connecticut.
The canoe carrying Tocci and another Holy Cross student capsized Saturday night, prompting a multi-day search.
The other student was rescued by a Good Samaritan and taken to a hospital; Tocci's death is under investigation.

title: Livestream: Testimony resumes in Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: John Waller
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a North Andover officer, faces trial for assault with a dangerous weapon after being shot by a colleague.
Fitzsimmons has pleaded not guilty to the charges related to the incident at her home last summer.
The trial's testimony is being livestreamed by NBC10 Boston as it resumes today.
